steps.title: Extract temperature values from the scatterplot
steps.content: This is not a modeling problem; it asks for the biggest one-step increase. Read the plotted points from left to right: $(1,69)$, $(2,60)$, $(3,73)$, $(4,68)$, $(5,64)$, $(6,62)$, and $(7,65)$.

steps.title: Calculate the temperature change for each interval
steps.content:
For each time period, compute $\Delta y = y_{\text{end}} - y_{\text{start}}$:
  • Choice D ($x=1$ to $x=2$): $60 - 69 = -9^{\circ}\text{F}$ (decrease)
  • Choice C ($x=2$ to $x=3$): $73 - 60 = 13^{\circ}\text{F}$ (increase)
  • Choice B ($x=5$ to $x=6$): $62 - 64 = -2^{\circ}\text{F}$ (decrease)
  • Choice A ($x=6$ to $x=7$): $65 - 62 = 3^{\circ}\text{F}$ (increase)
The greatest increase is $13^{\circ}\text{F}$ from day 2 to day 3. That is the correct answer.
